name: Dolly zoom / vertigo
description: Background scales up to 155% while the silhouette stays pinned. Fakes Hitchcock's FOV-swap lens move — great for tense reveals.

name: Pan left
description: Camera turns left, frame content slides to the right. 3800ms linear-ish ease. Plate sits at scale 1.15 so translate never exposes an edge.

name: Pan right
description: Camera turns right, frame content slides to the left. Mirror of pan-left. Use to follow subject direction or reveal something on the right.

name: Parallax depth (hover to drive)
description: Three layers shift at 0.5× / 1× / 2× of cursor offset. Title moves opposite. Fake-3D camera move done entirely in 2D — works for stills, hero titles, title backgrounds. Move your mouse over the card.

name: Slow pull-out
description: 3200ms scale from 110% → 100%, soft ease-out. Reveal move. Opens space around the subject — use when a shot needs to breathe or you're landing a beat.

name: Slow push-in
description: 3200ms scale from 100% → 110%, soft ease-out. The default storytelling move — use it on any hero still. Default move when you don't know what else to do.

name: Tilt down
description: Camera looks down, frame content slides up. Reveals floors, feet, the ground. Weighty — often used to land an emotional moment.

name: Tilt up
description: Camera looks up, frame content slides down. 3400ms. Reveals sky, ceilings, tall things. Classic "look who's here" move on a character.

name: Captions tool
description: Animated TikTok-style captions — paste a script, get cards with a hero word centred and supporting words tucked into the negative space around it. Three reveal modes, three hero-pick modes (asterisks / auto / Claude), MP4 export at 1080×1920 30fps via WebCodecs.

name: Bayer dither
description: Ordered 8×8 Bayer matrix quantises each pixel to a 1-bit ink/paper palette. The classic newspaper-print and zine reproduction look. Visible matrix grain is the giveaway. Pairs with Risograph Plum.
snippet_html:
Bayer dither

Ordered 8×8 Bayer matrix quantises each pixel to a 1-bit ink/paper palette. The classic newspaper-print and zine reproduction look. Visible matrix grain is the giveaway. Pairs with Risograph Plum.

Bleach Bypass

Desat -70%, contrast +75%. Crushed blacks, blown highlights, metallic feel — like the silver was left in the emulsion. AE: Lumetri → Saturation low, Contrast high. Ref: Saving Private Ryan, Seven.

name: Blue Hour

Blue Hour

Candlelight

name: Edge detection (Sobel)
description: 3×3 Sobel kernel on the luminance channel, applied per video frame. Every gradient becomes a line, every flat patch goes black. Editorial / TIWH stylisation pass; great as a transition state on the way to a graded shot. AE: Find Edges or CC Glass with low displacement; canvas here is the same maths.
